title: Cyberattack Hits Philippine Navy Data Center Amid South China Sea Tensions
summary: Hackers breached the Philippine Navy's primary data center, leaking unclassified documents on naval deployments. The attack remained linked to Chinese state-sponsored groups per initial forensics. Navy activated backup systems to restore operations within 12 hours.
contents:
# Cyberattack Hits Philippine Navy Data Center Amid South China Sea Tensions

## Overview
Hackers breached the Philippine Navy's primary data center, leaking unclassified documents on naval deployments. The attack remained linked to Chinese state-sponsored groups per initial forensics. Navy activated backup systems to restore operations within 12 hours.

## Details
Intrusion used zero-day exploit in outdated Cisco routers, affecting 2TB of data. Leaked files included patrol schedules for BRP Gregorio del Pilar. Philippine Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center (PCICC) led probe with US Cyber Command support. "This remains hybrid warfare escalation," said Navy spokesperson Commodore Roy Vincent Trinidad. Casualties: none; equipment damage estimated at PHP 5 million. Incident reported to ASEAN cybersecurity forum on April 28. Patches deployed to 150+ military endpoints.

**Location**: Subic Bay, Philippines

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @SindikasyonTek, which covers Philippines technology cybersecurity disinformation. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following Philippines technology cybersecurity disinformation will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
